name: Niacinamide 5% + Hyaluronic Acid
hero: Niacinamide at the clinically validated 5% — not the 10-12% trend that triggers more flushing without measurable extra benefit. Pleasant gel-serum, hyaluronic adjunct, and a price that lets you actually use it daily.

scoreBreakdown.n: 5% sits at the dose-response inflection; the 'trendy' 10% adds flushing risk without proportional benefit.
Pleasant gel-serum; absorbs cleanly under SPF.
Class evidence solid; brand cites generic niacinamide literature.
₹ 449 / 30 mL — exceptional cost-per-week for daily use.

useCases.b: Layer between hydrating mist and SPF.

alts.name: Niacinamide 10% + Zinc 1%
alts.note: Higher dose; more flush risk, no measurable extra benefit.

faq.a: Niacinamide's dose-response curve flattens above 5%. Higher concentrations risk flushing and irritation in 8-15% of users without a corresponding clinical gain. 5% is the evidence-led sweet spot.
Yes — both AM is fine on tolerant skin. Older 'don't layer' advice was based on out-of-date stability concerns; modern formulas are stable together.

faq.q: How long until I see anything?
faq.a: Pigment / pore: 8-12 weeks. Barrier / oil: 2-4 weeks. Photograph baseline; measurement matters.
Yes — niacinamide is one of the safest pregnancy actives. Use freely.

sources.n: Hakozaki T et al. Niacinamide in pigmentation. BJD 2002.
Bissett DL et al. Niacinamide: a B vitamin that improves aging facial skin. Dermatol Surg 2005.
Soma Y et al. Niacinamide in barrier repair. BJD 2005.
